title: Fix Select.Item empty value error in New Task dialog

requirements.text: In TaskDialog.tsx, fix the Select component so that no <Select.Item> has an empty string value prop. Replace any empty-string value option (used as a placeholder/default) with the correct Shadcn Select placeholder pattern: use the Select's placeholder prop or a disabled placeholder item with a non-empty sentinel value (e.g., 'none' or 'unassigned'), and update the form state and submission logic accordingly so that selecting the placeholder correctly maps to an empty/null value when submitting the task.

quotes: New tasks button shows error "A <Select.Item /> must have a value prop that is not an empty string. This is because the Select value can be set to an empty string to clear the selection and show the placeholder."

acceptanceCriteria: Opening the New Task dialog no longer produces the Select.Item empty value React error in the console.
All <Select.Item> components in TaskDialog.tsx have non-empty string value props.
The placeholder/default state for Select fields (e.g., asset, assignee) is handled via the Select placeholder prop or a sentinel value, not an empty string item.
Submitting a task with no asset or assignee selected correctly sends a null/empty optional value to the backend.
Existing task creation and editing functionality continues to work correctly end-to-end.

constraints: Do not modify any files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
Do not change the backend schema or any Motoko files.
nonGoals: Adding new fields or changing the task data model.
Modifying any page other than TaskDialog.tsx.
Any new features beyond this bug fix.
